perturbation.original_text: 3.2 Owner Default. Operator may terminate this Agreement at any time upon the occurrence of any of the following: (a) the dissolution or bankruptcy of Owner; (b) Owner fails to pay when due any amount due and payable hereunder, including, without limitation, any interest, fees, reimbursements or indemnifications, and such failure shall continue for a period of 15 days after the due date thereof; or (c) other than as set forth in clause (b) above, Owner breaches or fails to observe or perform any material term, condition, or obligation contained in this Agreement and fails to correct, or fails to diligently pursue correction of, such breach within 60 days after receipt of written notice from Operator of any such breach.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 Owner Default. Operator may terminate this Agreement at any time upon the occurrence of any of the following: (a) the dissolution or bankruptcy of Owner; (b) Owner fails to pay when due any amount due and payable hereunder, including, without limitation, any interest, fees, reimbursements or indemnifications, and such failure shall continue for a period of 30 days after the due date thereof; or (c) other than as set forth in clause (b) above, Owner breaches or fails to observe or perform any material term, condition, or obligation contained in this Agreement and fails to correct, or fails to diligently pursue correction of, such breach within 60 days after receipt of written notice from Operator of any such breach.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the grace period for Owner's failure to pay from 15 days to 30 days. This directly conflicts with Section 3.3(b) where Owner can terminate the agreement if the Operator fails to pay within 15 days of the due date. This change creates uncertainty about the actual grace period for payments and whether Owner and Operator should be held to different standards regarding late payments.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 Operator Default. Owner may terminate this Agreement at any time upon the occurrence of any of the following: (a) the dissolution or bankruptcy of Operator; (b) Operator fails to pay when due any amount due and payable hereunder, including, without limitation, any interest, fees, reimbursements or indemnifications, and such failure shall continue for a period of 15 days after the due date thereof; (c) other than as set forth in clause (b) above, Operator breaches or fails to observe or perform any material term, condition, or obligation contained in this Agreement and fails to correct, or fails to diligently pursue correction of, such breach within 60 days after receipt of written notice from Owner of any such breach; (d) Termination by Owner pursuant to Section 4.1.1; or (e) Without cause on no less than seven (7) days prior written notice. In the event Owner terminates this Agreement pursuant to subparagraph (e) above, Owner may reimburse Operator for any unamortized capital expenditure (determined in accordance with GAAP) to the extent such capital expenditure was included in the Operating and Capital Expenditure Budget. If any of the above occurs, other than an event described in subparagraph (d), and Owner elects to terminate this Agreement (a termination for "cause"), then Owner may give a written notice of termination to Operator, which termination shall be effective on the date specified by Owner in the notice, provided that such termination date shall be within 60 days of the date the notice is delivered to Operator. Owner's notice of breach to Operator under Section 3.3(b) or (c) shall state with particularity the breach alleged by Owner. To the extent Operator disputes the basis for Owner's notice of breach, the matter shall be addressed under Section 8.6. Nothing in this Section 3.3 shall be construed to limit or preclude any remedy Owner may have at law or in equity with respect to any material breach by Operator.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the grace period for Owner's failure to pay from 15 days to 30 days in Section 3.2(b), while the contradicted text in Section 3.3(b) states that Owner can terminate the agreement if the Operator fails to pay within 15 days of the due date. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the grace period for payments, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1.2 Improvements/Alterations to the Rail Facility. Operator shall evaluate and modify, at its sole cost and expense, the rail car containment pan system (the "Containment Pan System") so that such system is in compliance with the Spill Prevention, Control, and Countermeasure Regulations promulgated by the Environmental Protection Agency (the "SPCC Regulations") and any other applicable regulations, rules or similar administrative publications promulgated by any other federal, state, or local regulatory agency. Such evaluation and modification, if necessary, of the Containment Pan System shall occur within ninety (90) days of the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1.2 Improvements/Alterations to the Rail Facility. Operator shall evaluate and modify, at Owner's sole cost and expense, the rail car containment pan system (the "Containment Pan System") so that such system is in compliance with the Spill Prevention, Control, and Countermeasure Regulations promulgated by the Environmental Protection Agency (the "SPCC Regulations") and any other applicable regulations, rules or similar administrative publications promulgated by any other federal, state, or local regulatory agency. Such evaluation and modification, if necessary, of the Containment Pan System shall occur within ninety (90) days of the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by shifting the financial responsibility for modifications to the Containment Pan System from the Operator to the Owner. This contradicts section 4.1.4, which states that the Operator agrees to keep and maintain the Rail Facility in a condition and repair similar to its condition on the Effective Date at the Operator's sole cost and expense. This change creates uncertainty about who is financially responsible for the Containment Pan System's upgrades.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1.4 Maintenance of the Rail Facility. Subject to the terms, conditions and limitations set forth in this Agreement, Owner hereby authorizes and empowers Operator, and Operator agrees, in the name of and on behalf of Owner, to, at its sole cost and expense, keep and maintain the Rail Facility in a condition and repair similar to, but not less than, its condition and repair on the Effective Date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text shifts the cost of the rail car containment pan system modification from the Operator to the Owner. This directly contradicts section 4.1.4, which states that the Operator is responsible for maintaining the Rail Facility at its sole cost and expense. This creates a clear inconsistency within the contract regarding financial responsibility for the Containment Pan System, which falls under maintenance and improvements of the rail facility as a whole. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction: different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 8.13 Assignment. Operator shall not make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder unless there first shall have been obtained the written consent thereto of Owner, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed. Owner shall not make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder except in connection with the sale, financing or conveyance of all or any part of the Rail Facility.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.13 Assignment. Operator shall not make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder. Owner shall not make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder except in connection with the sale, financing or conveyance of all or any part of the Rail Facility.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by omitting the clause indicating the owner's written consent must not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed for the operator's assignment requests. This contradicts the spirit of mutual cooperation and fairness implied throughout the agreement, introducing ambiguity over the conditions under which assignment is allowed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.13 Assignment. Operator shall not make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder. Owner shall not make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder except in connection with the sale, financing or conveyance of all or any part of the Rail Facility. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the successors and assigns of the Parties. Any attempted assignment of this Agreement in violation of this Section 8.12 shall be null and void.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the Owner's consent should not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed. The changed text removes this clause, creating a scenario where the Owner could unreasonably withhold consent, directly contradicting the implied fairness within the agreement. The contradicted text contains the changed text that eliminates the aforementioned clause. Therefore, the change is substantial enough to cause confusion and introduce uncertainty regarding the conditions of assignment, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation.
